pattern: Interval dynamic programming counts minimum print passes, exploiting that a run printed for the left end can be extended for free wherever the same character reappears.
hints: Collapsing consecutive duplicate characters never changes the answer, so reduce runs to single characters first.
Think of the cost to print a substring [i..j]; the key saving is when s[i] equals some s[k] inside the range.

Let dp[i][j] be the min turns for substring i..j. Base dp[i][i]=1. dp[i][j] starts as 1 + dp[i+1][j]; for each k in (i, j] with s[k]==s[i], try dp[i+1][k-1] + dp[k][j] to merge the print of s[i] with s[k].

edge_inputs.description: outer character reappears, saving a turn

stress.description: random short strings over a tiny alphabet to create overlap opportunities
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(1, max(2, min(size, 40)))
    alpha = 'abc'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
stress.note: small alphabet and length keep the O(n^3) DP fast while exercising overlaps
